data.title: Pinewood Technologies: Apax Withdrawal Creates Attractive Entry Point Amid Strong Long-Term Earnings Potential
data.description:
Pinewood Technologies, the Technology sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Andrew Wade from Jefferies maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a p550.00 price target.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Andrew Wade has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, notably his view that Pinewood Technologies can grow meaningfully despite recent volatility and sector worries. While the withdrawal of Apax’s possible 500p per share offer triggered a sharp share price decline, he sees this as creating an attractive entry point rather than a deterioration in fundamentals.

He acknowledges industry debate around potential AI disruption but concludes that Pinewood is still well positioned to expand earnings over the coming years. Even after applying a more conservative peer-based valuation multiple, his analysis indicates substantial upside from the current price, supporting the decision to resume coverage with a Buy recommendation.

In another report released on January 31, TipRanks – Google also upgraded the stock to a Buy with a p548.00 price target.

data.title: Expand Energy: Strong 4Q25 Beat, Enhanced 2026 Capital Efficiency, and Robust Free Cash Flow Outlook Support Buy Rating
data.description:
William Blair analyst Neal Dingmann has maintained their bullish stance on EXE stock, giving a Buy rating on February 13.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Neal Dingmann has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Expand Energy’s stronger-than-anticipated fourth-quarter 2025 EBITDAX and free cash flow that exceeded both his and the Street’s expectations, largely on the back of better natural gas volumes and improved NGL realizations. He also views the modestly upgraded 2026 outlook favorably, as management now plans slightly lower capital spending while keeping production targets intact, which enhances capital efficiency and supports durable cash generation.

In his view, the company’s steady development and completion program, coupled with a clear priority on debt reduction rather than share repurchases, positions Expand Energy to generate more than $2.5 billion of free cash flow in 2026 and further strengthen its balance sheet. Given the earnings beat, the capital discipline, and the constructive multi‑year cash flow profile, Dingmann anticipates EXE shares will outperform peers in the near term, reinforcing his positive stance on the stock.

In another report released on February 13, Siebert Williams Shank & Co also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $148.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
